title: Falcode Theme
description: Color theme for the falcode terminal multiplexer. Place this file at ~/.config/falcode/themes/{name}.json and set theme: "{name}" in your falcode config.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this schema file.

defs.description: Named color aliases. Values in 'theme' that match a key here are replaced with the alias value.

theme.description: Color tokens for each UI element. Each token must define both a dark and a light value. Values can be a hex color or a key from 'defs'.

workspace_active.description: Background of the active workspace tab.

workspace_active_fg.description: Foreground (text) of the active workspace tab.

workspace_bar_bg.description: Background of the outer workspace tab bar.

workspace_border.description: Border color of the active workspace.

prefix_indicator_fg.description: Color of the prefix-mode indicator shown in the footer.

status_fg.description: Color used for status/error messages in the footer.

inner_active.description: Background of the active inner tab.

inner_active_fg.description: Foreground (text) of the active inner tab.

inner_bar_bg.description: Background of the inner tab bar row.

inner_separator.description: Color of the separator between inner tabs.

sheet_bg.description: Background of the which-key overlay sheet.

sheet_border.description: Border color of the which-key overlay sheet.

sheet_title.description: Color of the title text in the which-key sheet.

sheet_key.description: Color of key labels in the which-key sheet.

sheet_description.description: Color of description text in the which-key sheet.

sheet_group.description: Color of group/section headings in the which-key sheet.

sheet_separator.description: Color of separator lines in the which-key sheet.

colorPair.description: A color with both dark and light scheme variants.

dark.description: Color used when the dark scheme is active. A hex color (#RRGGBB), a key from 'defs', or "transparent" to inherit the terminal default.

light.description: Color used when the light scheme is active. A hex color (#RRGGBB), a key from 'defs', or "transparent" to inherit the terminal default.
